title: Diffusion for Audio Generation
concept: Diffusion models can generate spectrograms or raw waveforms directly, applying the same noise-then-denoise process used for images.

exercises.prompt: What do you think happens when you slowly add random noise (static) to a photo until it's all fuzzy?

options.text: The photo becomes pure random noise, hiding the original picture
The photo turns into a completely different photo
Nothing happens, the photo stays the same

exercises.explain: Yes! If you keep adding noise, the picture eventually turns into total random static. Diffusion models learn to reverse this process step by step.

cards.title: Can AI Sculpt Sound from Static?
cards.body: Imagine turning on the radio and hearing pure hissing static... then slowly, out of that noise, a clear song or voice emerges! That's basically what diffusion models do to create audio. They start with random noise and carve out music, speech, or sound effects step by step.

cards.title: What Is Audio Diffusion?
cards.body: Diffusion for audio is a way for AI to create sound by starting with random noise and slowly cleaning it up, little by little, until it turns into a clear spectrogram (a picture of sound) or a raw waveform (the actual sound wave). It's the same trick used for generating images, just applied to sound instead.

cards.title: The Noisy Sculptor
cards.body: Think of a sculptor staring at a rough, messy block of marble full of random bumps. Instead of adding clay, they carefully chip away tiny bits of noise over and over. Step by step, a smooth, beautiful statue appears. Audio diffusion works the same way, but instead of marble, it starts with random noise and 'chips away' until clean sound appears.

cards.title: From Static to Song
cards.body: An AI music tool starts with a screen full of random noisy dots (like TV static, but representing sound). Step 1: mostly noise, tiny hints of rhythm. Step 20: you can hear faint beats. Step 50: a full, clear piano melody plays. Each step removes a little more noise, revealing the sound hidden inside.

cards.title: Myth: Diffusion Only Works for Pictures
cards.body: Myth: diffusion models can only generate images since they were designed for pictures. Truth: the exact same noise-then-denoise idea works for spectrograms (sound pictures) and even raw waveforms. AI doesn't care if the data is a picture of a cat or a picture of sound, it just learns patterns and removes noise the same way.

cards.title: Image Diffusion vs Audio Diffusion
cards.body: Both processes follow the exact same noise-then-denoise steps, just with different kinds of data being cleaned up.

left.text: Starts with noisy pixels, denoises step by step into a clear picture like a face or landscape

right.text: Starts with noisy spectrogram or waveform, denoises step by step into clear music or speech

exercises.prompt: What does an audio diffusion model start with before creating sound?

options.text: A finished song it copies exactly

exercises.explain: Correct! Just like image diffusion starts with noisy pixels, audio diffusion starts with random noise and gradually removes it to reveal sound.

exercises.prompt: Diffusion models can only be used to generate images, never sound.
exercises.explain: False! Diffusion models can generate spectrograms or raw waveforms too, using the same noise-then-denoise process used for images.

exercises.sentence: Audio diffusion models generate sound by starting with random noise and slowly ___ it step by step.
exercises.explain: The model gradually removes noise, step by step, until clear audio (like music or speech) appears.

exercises.prompt: A spectrogram is best described as...

options.text: A file that stores only silence

exercises.explain: A spectrogram turns sound into a visual picture, which is why image-style diffusion techniques can be applied directly to it.

exercises.prompt: Which statement correctly explains how diffusion models generate audio?

options.text: They record real audio and slightly rearrange the order of sounds
They start with random noise and gradually denoise it into a spectrogram or waveform, just like image diffusion does with pixels
They require a human to manually draw every sound wave first
They only work if given an existing song to copy note-for-note

exercises.explain: Exactly! Audio diffusion uses the same core idea as image diffusion: start with noise, then denoise step by step, just applied to sound data like spectrograms or waveforms instead of pixels.
